verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1/Hsp72) is canonically a cytosolic molecular chaperone lacking a TM domain, GPI anchor, or secretory signal. However, extensive gene-specific experimental evidence — including flow cytometry on live non-permeabilized tumor cells (cmHsp70.1 mAb detecting extracellular-exposed TKD epitope), surface biotinylation, confocal imaging, and membrane proteomics — documents its stable integration into the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in tumor cells (via Gb3/lipid raft and PS interactions) and in stressed cells recovering from heat shock. This membrane pool is wash-resistant (not removed by high/low salt or pH changes), placing it above a simple reversible recruitment threshold. Surface expression is absent in normal, non-stressed, non-tumor tissues tested across ~1,000 primary biopsies. The surface display is thus conditional on oncogenic transformation or cellular stress (heat shock, hypoxia, nutrient deprivation), qualifying as cell_state_induced. This is a genuine extracellular-face exposure with clinical antibody (cmHsp70.1) and CAR-NK therapeutic programs targeting the surface pool.
